creator: Amanda Silberling
title: (No AI) Hey Siri, here’s what I actually want from AI
dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
content: I'm desperate for a personal AI assistant, but do I really want to become the kind of person who can't function without the friendly robot voice in my phone?
contentSnippet: I'm desperate for a personal AI assistant, but do I really want to become the kind of person who can't function without the friendly robot voice in my phone?

title: (No AI) GM thinks EVs can help offset AI’s energy suck with vehicle-to-grid tech
author: Andrew J. Hawkins
content:
![GM vehicle to grid](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/GM-Energy-home-energy-system-2.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

At an event in San Francisco today, General Motors made a series of announcements around EV batteries, energy storage, and grid resiliency in the face of [growing electricity demand from AI data centers](https://www.theverge.com/news/845831/ai-chips-data-center-power-water). The automaker announced that it would be activating new vehicle-to-grid capabilities for its current EV and home energy customers. It's releasing a new commercial energy storage system strategy, anchored by newly developed sodium-ion batteries for industrial-scale grid applications. And it's launching a new feature for EV owners that it says will help simplify public charging.

Right now, millions of EVs are sitting idly in driveways across …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/transportation/946820/gm-energy-ev-v2g-storage-sodium-ion)
contentSnippet:
At an event in San Francisco today, General Motors made a series of announcements around EV batteries, energy storage, and grid resiliency in the face of growing electricity demand from AI data centers. The automaker announced that it would be activating new vehicle-to-grid capabilities for its current EV and home energy customers. It's releasing a new commercial energy storage system strategy, anchored by newly developed sodium-ion batteries for industrial-scale grid applications. And it's launching a new feature for EV owners that it says will help simplify public charging. 
Right now, millions of EVs are sitting idly in driveways across  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The automaker announced that it would be activating new vehicle-to-grid capabilities for its current EV and home energy customers. It's releasing a […]

title: (No AI) Microsoft AI head calls out Anthropic for acting like Claude is conscious
author: Emma Roth
content:
![A photo of Mustafa Suleyman](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/gettyimages-2207866106.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman says it's "really, really dangerous" for Anthropic to speculate about Claude's consciousness [inside its "constitution,"](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/865185/anthropic-claude-constitution-soul-doc) or the instructions that tell the model how to behave. During [an episode of _Decoder_](https://www.theverge.com/podcast/944138/microsoft-ai-ceo-mustafa-suleyman-superintelligence-agi-openai-automation), Suleyman argues that this kind of speculation may have set up the chatbot to act as though it's conscious:

> I think that it's almost as though some of the folks at Anthropic have anthropomorphized the design of Claude so much that it has then gone and wireheaded them and kind of tricked them into believing that it has these glimmers of consciousness that they put into it in the first place.

> [
> 
> View …
> 
> 
> 
> ](https://www.instagram.com/reel/DZXp12HDXdN/?utm_source=ig_embed&utm_campaign=loading)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/947197/microsoft-ai-mustafa-suleyman-anthropic-claude-conscious)
contentSnippet:
Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman says it's "really, really dangerous" for Anthropic to speculate about Claude's consciousness inside its "constitution," or the instructions that tell the model how to behave. During an episode of Decoder, Suleyman argues that this kind of speculation may have set up the chatbot to act as though it's conscious:
I think that it's almost as though some of the folks at Anthropic have anthropomorphized the design of Claude so much that it has then gone and wireheaded them and kind of tricked them into believing that it has these glimmers of consciousness that they put into it in the first place.

creator: Katie Drummond
title: (No AI) Alex Vindman Survived Trump’s Retaliation Machine. Now He’s Running for Senate
dc:creator: Katie Drummond
content: In 2019, Alex Vindman testified during President Trump’s first impeachment trial–a decision that ended his military career. Now he wants to challenge the president from the halls of Congress.
contentSnippet: In 2019, Alex Vindman testified during President Trump’s first impeachment trial–a decision that ended his military career. Now he wants to challenge the president from the halls of Congress.
